---
applyTo: "**/*"
description: "Shared CDC validation guidance for Copilot and other coding agents."
---

Use this guidance when working anywhere in the repository.

- Treat the project as a CDC validation system, not a public API ingestion system.
- Assume source data is generated by stored procedures in PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
- Keep CDC validation logic read-only.
- Compare CDC data from OLTP sources to the PostgreSQL OLAP warehouse.
- Use warehouse load audit tables and centralized Azure logs when diagnosing failures.
- Favor simple validation checks first: row counts, missing keys, duplicate keys, CDC checkpoint freshness, operation counts, and null counts.
- Keep changes minimal and consistent with the existing architecture docs.
